prerequisites: A Missive account that is part of an organization subscribed to the Productive plan, which is required to generate API tokens.
A Missive API token created from the API tab in your Missive preferences (used as the connector's api_key).
A start_date value to bound how far back the connector reads data.
Optional: a kind value to scope which resource is pulled, and a limit value to cap the number of records returned.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Missive uses a personal API token sent as a Bearer credential in the Authorization HTTP header (Authorization: Bearer <token>, tokens are prefixed missive_pat-). Provide this token as the connector's api_key secret field. Tokens are personal, not organization-wide, and grant access to any account the issuing user can reach in Missive.

setupSteps.title: Confirm Productive plan access
setupSteps.body: Generating Missive API tokens requires being part of an organization subscribed to Missive's Productive plan. Confirm your organization is on this plan before continuing.

setupSteps.title: Create an API token in Missive
setupSteps.body: Open your Missive preferences, select the API tab, and click 'Create a new token'. Copy the generated token (it begins with missive_pat-). Tokens are personal and inherit access to every account the issuing user can reach in Missive.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token as api_key
setupSteps.body: Paste the token into the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header on each REST API request.

setupSteps.title: Configure the sync parameters
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date to define how far back the connector reads. Optionally set kind to scope the resource being pulled and limit to cap the number of records returned per request.
